member.name: Edward Morello
member.synopsis: Edward Morello is the Liberal Democrat MP for West Dorset , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what discussions his Department has had with rail operators on service reliability caused by climate change affecting West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department engages regularly with Network Rail and the Train Operating Companies (TOCs) on the impact of extreme weather and climate change on rail service reliability across the network, including on routes serving rural areas such as West Dorset. Ne...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 2 July 2026 to Question 14261, how much funding her Department is providing under the Bus Service Operators Grant to West Dorset; and whether her Department plans to provide ring fence
writtenQuestions.answerText: BSOG is paid directly to eligible bus and community transport operators, rather than being allocated by constituency. The Department for Transport does not hold information on the amount of Bus Service Operators Grant (BSOG) funding provided specifically ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Transport: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment his Department has made of the impact of community transport on rural mobility in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Bus services can be a lifeline in rural areas and can often be the only means of accessing essential services. The Government is, therefore, helping improve local bus services across the country, including through the Bus Services Act 2025, which puts the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department plans to review police funding formulas to reflect rurality.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Police Reform White Paper confirmed that we will review the funding formula once police reform is underway.

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness of rural policing in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Police Reform White Paper confirmed that we will review the funding formula once police reform is underway.

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to support policing in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Police Reform White Paper confirmed that we will review the funding formula once police reform is underway.

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment his Department has made of economic growth opportunities in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DBT works closely with other Government Departments and local partner organisations, including Local Authorities, to collect intelligence and data, identify issues, and support the development and delivery of initiatives to open up opportunities for econo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to support seasonal hospitality businesses in rural constituencies such as West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are delivering permanently lower business rates multipliers for over 750,000 retail, hospitality and leisure properties, backed by a £4.3 billion support package over three years, including transitional relief to protect businesses from large bill incr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Houses: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what support his Department is providing to pubs in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since April 2026, every pub has benefited from a 15% cut to their business rates bills, followed by a two year real terms freeze. Around three‑quarters of pubs will see their bills stay the same or fall, saving the average pub around £1,650 this year. A r...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment his Department has made of the impact of rising costs on hospitality businesses in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that hospitality businesses, including those in West Dorset, continue to face cost pressures arising from wider economic conditions. While the Department has not made a specific assessment for West Dorset, we remain in regular di...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Houses: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department plans to review support available for rural pubs in constituencies such as West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department keeps the adequacy of support for rural pubs under regular review, working closely with HM Treasury and industry representatives including through the Hospitality Sector Council. Since April 2026, every pub has benefited from a 15% cut to t...

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent support his Department is providing to hospitality businesses in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are delivering permanently lower business rates multipliers for over 750,000 retail, hospitality and leisure properties, backed by a £4.3 billion support package over three years, including transitional relief to protect businesses from large bill incr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Houses: West Dorset
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment his Department has made of the financial sustainability of pubs in West Dorset.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment of the financial sustainability of pubs in West Dorset. However, the Government recognises the pressures facing pubs and the wider hospitality sector and continues to engage closely with industry represent...

writtenQuestions.heading: Solar Power: Innovation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to support innovation in the solar sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has huge potential for solar power, and the Government is committed to working with industry to radically increase our existing solar capacity to meet our Net Zero goals utilising innovation funded through UK Research & Innovation and Innovate ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department works closely with train operators and Network Rail to improve reliability and performance on services across the network, including those serving West Dorset. Improvement activity includes targeted work to address infrastructure reliabilit...
